title: The Duel

jokes.text: 12 miles an hour. eat that, carl lewis!
jokes.setup: Radar gun shows someone running slowly at 12 mph
jokes.punchline: Jim dramatically compares his pathetic speed to Olympic sprinter Carl Lewis
jokes.explanation: Strong economy and character integration - Jim's deadpan delivery of absurdly misplaced confidence is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: 12 miles per hour? That's... that's Carl Lewis speed right there.

jokes.text: Angela made several 9-1-1 calls about cars going too fast in front of the building, so the police put up a radar gun. it's actually caused a bit of a traffic hazard.
jokes.setup: Angela complained about speeding cars as a safety issue
jokes.punchline: The solution (radar gun) creates the exact problem it was meant to solve
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela character integration - her uptight nature backfiring is earned comedy

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: So Angela complains about speeding cars being dangerous, right? So we get a radar gun to catch the speeders. But now everyone's driving recklessly trying to see how fast they can go past it.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: That's not how radar guns work.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: I know, but it's funny to watch you get upset about it.

jokes.text: wow, 13! no, no. no, there was wind. i was just jogging. dwight, there was wind.
jokes.setup: Jim trying to excuse his slow radar gun speed
jokes.punchline: Desperate attempts to justify 13 mph as acceptable running speed
jokes.explanation: Jim's rare moment of being flustered and making excuses is good character work

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Look, 13 mph is a respectable speed. That's like, jogging speed.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: That's not jogging speed, Jim. That's shuffling speed.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Well, I'm not a professional runner. I'm an office worker.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: My grandmother runs faster than that.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, your grandmother is very fit, Pam.

jokes.text: 31! 31! there was a car. i was ahead of the car. 31 is my new number.
jokes.setup: Office competition for radar gun speeds
jokes.punchline: Dwight claiming impossible speed by running with traffic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight - his competitive nature and inability to accept defeat leads to absurd claim

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I clocked myself at 60 miles per hour. I was running with traffic.

jokes.text: 31 is humanly impossible.
jokes.setup: Dwight's impossible speed claim
jokes.punchline: Jim's matter-of-fact response to obvious lie
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim straight-man reaction - perfect economy and delivery

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I can run 60 miles per hour.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: No you can't.

jokes.text: he says that he wants to talk about big picture stuff. and i'll be honest, i have little or no idea what that means, so... probably bad.
jokes.setup: Michael called to corporate for mysterious 'big picture' meeting
jokes.punchline: His immediate assumption that confusion equals doom
jokes.explanation: Michael's insecurity and tendency to catastrophize is perfectly captured

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: They called me up to corporate for some big picture meeting. I don't know what it's about.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: But whenever they call you in for a meeting and they don't tell you what it's about, that means you're fired.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm getting fired. I know it.

jokes.text: new year, new candy. whoo-hoo! okay, be careful, kevin. they're kind of spicy. hot tamales. yeah. so maybe just try one at first, and then if it's okay, have a couple more.
jokes.setup: Michael announcing new office candy
jokes.punchline: Treating Kevin like a child who can't handle mild candy
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's patronizing nature and Kevin's perceived incompetence

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Everyone, I have an announcement. We're getting a new candy in the office.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Oh yeah? What kind?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: It's a spicy cinnamon candy. Nothing too intense.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Kevin, I'm going to need you to sit down for this one.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Why? I like spicy stuff.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm going to get you some milk. And maybe a juice box.

jokes.text: no one has rsvp'd to our wedding yet, and the deadline was yesterday.
jokes.setup: Andy announcing wedding RSVP status to office
jokes.punchline: Complete lack of responses reveals office's knowledge of affair
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe setup - Andy's obliviousness creates painful dramatic irony

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Okay everyone, I'm sending out wedding invitations and I need RSVPs by Friday.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: So far I have... zero responses.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Not even a 'maybe.' Just complete silence.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I guess nobody's coming to my wedding.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Well, they know about you and Erin.

jokes.text: andy still doesn't know that angela's having an affair with dwight. and it's been 17 days. i mean, eventually he'll figure it out... when their kids have giant heads and beet-stained teeth.
jokes.setup: Jim explaining Andy's obliviousness in talking head
jokes.punchline: Imagining future children with Dwight's distinctive features
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual comedy through description - 'beet-stained teeth' is particularly inspired and character-specific

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Dwight's gonna have kids someday, and I just... I picture them, and they're gonna have like, these really intense eyebrows, and they're gonna be very serious about farming.

jokes.text: how can he still not know?
jokes.setup: Continued discussion of Andy's obliviousness
jokes.punchline: Jim's genuine bewilderment at Andy's denseness
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat that works but isn't particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: I don't even know what to say. Like, how is he not getting this?

jokes.text: what is wrong with you? she is engaged. did you ever have intercourse in this office?
jokes.setup: Jim confronting Dwight about the affair
jokes.punchline: Escalation from moral outrage to invasive workplace questioning
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure, uncomfortable workplace boundary crossing

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Dwight, I can't believe you would do this. You're supposed to be a man of principles.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: My personal life is none of your concern, Jim.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: None of my concern? You're making a mockery of everything this office stands for. Angela trusted you!

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Angela and I have an understanding.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: An understanding? Dwight, I need to know the specifics. How many times? Where? Did you use protection? What were the ambient temperatures? I need details.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Why would I tell you any of that?

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Because I'm very upset, and I need to know everything about your sexual encounters to process my emotions properly.

jokes.text: are you serious? ugh. where?
jokes.setup: Dwight's implication that they did have office intercourse
jokes.punchline: Jim's disgust mixed with morbid curiosity
jokes.explanation: Jim's conflicted reaction - disgusted but can't help asking for details

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Wait, what? No! Absolutely not.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Oh come on, Jim. You're telling me you never thought about it?

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: No! I... why would you even say that?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Because I saw the way you looked at me during the fire drill.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: That was disgust, Dwight. Pure disgust. And... okay, I'm a little curious now, but no.

jokes.text: where, dwight? seems like you already know where.
jokes.setup: Jim demanding location of office intercourse
jokes.punchline: Dwight's response implies it was obvious/visible location
jokes.explanation: Implies visual gag of Jim realizing where it happened - uncomfortable recognition

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Where did you and Angela do it?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: On top of the filing cabinets.

jokes.text: oh, i'm sorry. i didn't realize i was doing something wrong. if i had, i would've admitted it and stopped right away. because i wouldn't want an innocent person who doesn't know anything about the form--
jokes.setup: Kevin responding to Angela's criticism about form errors
jokes.punchline: Kevin's 'innocent' response is clearly about Angela's affair, not forms
jokes.explanation: Brilliant double-meaning dialogue - Kevin's revenge through passive-aggressive form criticism metaphor

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, Angela. I didn't know the forms were so important to you. I guess some people just have a hard time keeping their personal life separate from their work life.

jokes.text: that was good. it just at the end, you weren't saying something that could also apply to the form. how about 'i'm sorry i did such a whorish job filling out this form.'
jokes.setup: Kevin's metaphorical dig at Angela
jokes.punchline: Oscar coaching Kevin to be more explicit in his double-meaning insult
jokes.explanation: Oscar's analytical approach to insult construction is perfectly in character - treating it like a writing exercise

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Angela, you're like a vending machine. You take my money and give me nothing.

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: No, no, Kevin. You have to be more direct. Tell her what you really mean.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: I... I don't know what you mean.

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: You're saying she's cold, unfeeling, a waste of your time and money. Say it like you mean it.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Angela, you're a vending machine and you suck.

jokes.text: that cannot be true! you're going to charge me a fee to cut my own cake? uh, no. no, no, no. what i'm saying is i want to cut it myself.
jokes.setup: Andy planning wedding details
jokes.punchline: His outrage at cake-cutting fee reveals his cheapness despite wealthy background
jokes.explanation: Shows Andy's entitled but penny-pinching nature

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. They're charging me $75 to cut the cake? I'm not paying $75 to cut a cake. I can cut a cake.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: My grandmother cut cakes for 40 years. I think I can handle this.

jokes.text: trade seats with me. no. i've got a better angle on pam. i can see everything. please stop.
jokes.setup: Kevin wanting to switch seats
jokes.punchline: Kevin's creepy admission about watching Pam, Oscar's discomfort
jokes.explanation: Kevin's inappropriate behavior creates discomfort - more uncomfortable than funny

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Can I switch seats with you, Pam? I like to watch you work.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Um... why?

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: It's just... the way you type. The way you answer the phone. You're like a beautiful, efficient machine.

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: Okay, that's weird, man.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: What? I'm complimenting her!

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: Yeah, in a serial killer way.

jokes.text: rule 17. don't turn your back on bears, men you have wronged, or the dominant turkey during mating season. there are 40 rules all schrute boys must learn before the age of five.
jokes.setup: Dwight sensing danger and reciting family wisdom
jokes.punchline: Bizarre specific rule mixing genuine threats with random turkey fact
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight - mixing practical survival advice with absurd specificity about turkey behavior

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: A Schrute never leaves his post unattended. A Schrute never cries. A Schrute never admits defeat. And a Schrute never eats a turkey that weighs more than 300 pounds, because the legs will snap right off and it's a mess.

jokes.text: learn your rules, you better learn your rules, if you don't, you'll be eaten in your sleep
jokes.setup: Dwight's childhood rule system
jokes.punchline: Ominous sing-song rhyme about being eaten for rule violations
jokes.explanation: Dwight's childhood trauma expressed through sinister nursery rhyme - perfectly bizarre

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: When I was a child, my father instituted a very strict rule system in our house. If you broke a rule, you had to spin the Wheel of Punishment.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: One time I didn't do my chores, so I had to spin it. I landed on 'be eaten.'

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: My father said, 'You break the rules, you get eaten. You break the rules, you get eaten. You break the rules, you get eaten.' He sang it like that, in a very ominous sing-song voice.

jokes.text: you've got to tell andy about us. that is a terrible idea. one of your worst. get it over with. then we don't have to hide anymore. you're expanding on your worst idea.
jokes.setup: Angela wanting to come clean to Andy
jokes.punchline: Dwight's escalating assessment of how bad her idea is
jokes.explanation: Dwight's blunt escalation of criticism is in character - practical but harsh

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: I think I should tell Andy the truth.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: That's a bad idea.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: That's a terrible idea.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: That's the worst idea anyone has ever had.

jokes.text: do you love me or not? i've already admitted that i do. why do you keep making me repeat it? because you're engaged to andy.
jokes.setup: Angela demanding love declarations
jokes.punchline: Dwight's logical response highlighting the obvious problem
jokes.explanation: Dwight's matter-of-fact response to Angela's emotional manipulation

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: I need you to tell me you love me.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I love you.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: No, I need you to *mean* it.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I do mean it. I'm very literal. If I didn't mean it, I wouldn't have said it.

jokes.text: is she crazy in bed? yes. stop. how so, specifically? what? okay, listen. eager and flexible.
jokes.setup: Jim asking inappropriate questions about Angela
jokes.punchline: Dwight immediately providing specific details despite protests
jokes.explanation: Dwight's inability to not answer personal questions creates uncomfortable comedy

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: So Dwight, how often would you say you and Angela... you know?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: That's inappropriate, Jim.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Come on, just give me a ballpark figure.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I'm not going to—

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Yeah, Dwight, we're all curious.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Fine. Tuesdays and Thursdays. Sometimes Saturdays. We maintain a schedule to optimize—

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: That's... that's exactly what I was asking for.

jokes.text: i am already walking. michael, once this gets out, i don't know how it's gonna go down. okay, what does that mean? might get ugly.
jokes.setup: Michael warning about potential consequences
jokes.punchline: Vague ominous warning that sounds more dramatic than it is
jokes.explanation: Michael's tendency to be dramatic about minor workplace issues

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm gonna say this once, and I'm gonna say it clearly. What you're about to do has consequences. Serious consequences. And I need you to understand that when you cross this line, there's no going back. Do you understand me?

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Yeah, Michael, I'm just gonna grab a donut from the break room.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Then you know what you're dealing with.

jokes.text: and just bear in mind that when i say-- say these things... that are bad things that you hear... in your ears, this is something that i... if i were you, that i wouldn't want to hear. you're not making any sense.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to break bad news to Andy
jokes.punchline: His complete inability to form coherent sentences under pressure
jokes.explanation: Michael's anxiety-induced word salad is perfectly in character - shows his good intentions but poor execution

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Andy, I need to tell you something and I'm just going to rip the band-aid off. Your... your performance review is... well, it's not great. Actually, it's bad. No, it's... look, the numbers don't lie, and the numbers are saying that you're not... you're underperforming in the... the areas.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: What? Michael, what are you talking about?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm saying that your... your synergy with the team is at an all-time... it's low. Very low. And your quarterly objectives, they're not being... objectified. I mean achieved. They're not being achieved.

jokes.text: well... no, i'm not. so i-- i'm not very articulate today, so i'll just leave it for another time. another day. which'll be fine. i'm off. have a good meeting. thank you. kick wallace's ass. okay, i will.
jokes.setup: Michael chickening out of telling Andy the truth
jokes.punchline: His awkward retreat and Andy's oblivious encouragement
jokes.explanation: Michael's cowardice and Andy's continued obliviousness create cringe moment

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Andy, I need to tell you something important about your performance review...

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Yes, Michael? I'm ready to hear it.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Well, the thing is... you're... I mean... have you considered that... um...

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Michael, whatever it is, I can take it. I'm a professional.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Right, right. Professional. That's great. You know what? You're doing great. Really great. I'm going to go... make a call.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Wait, you got this, Michael! You're a great leader!

jokes.text: dwight and angela are having an affair, so. i can't hear you through the glass. dwight and angela are having an affair. they've been sleeping together for some time.
jokes.setup: Michael finally blurting out the truth as his car drives away
jokes.punchline: Andy can't hear, forcing Michael to repeat it louder and more explicitly
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic structure - the thing Michael was afraid to say gets repeated and amplified

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm in love with you!

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm in love with you!

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I can't hear you!

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I SAID I'M IN LOVE WITH YOU!

jokes.text: i knew something bad was gonna happen today. you said that yesterday. yeah, my neighbor got murdered.
jokes.setup: David noting Michael's recurring pessimism
jokes.punchline: Michael's matter-of-fact revelation about actual murder
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - what seems like paranoia is revealed to be completely justified. Perfect economy.

dialogue.speaker: David Wallace
dialogue.line: You know, Michael, you're always so pessimistic about everything.

dialogue.speaker: Michael Scott
dialogue.line: Well, David, that's because I've actually murdered someone.

jokes.text: what are you standing for? if i'm sitting, i can't disable his neck or his groin. you're not gonna do anything to his neck or his groin. if i'm sitting, i don't have the option to.
jokes.setup: Jim asking why Dwight is standing
jokes.punchline: Dwight's tactical reasoning for maintaining attack options
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight logic - treating office work as combat situation requiring tactical positioning

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Why are you standing?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: It's a power position. Gives me tactical advantage. I can attack from here, or I can run. Sitting leaves you vulnerable.

jokes.text: would i have said yes to formal chrysanthemums if i didn't want to get married?
jokes.setup: Angela defending her commitment to Andy
jokes.punchline: Using flower choice as evidence of wedding sincerity while admitting to affair
jokes.explanation: Angela's twisted logic and obsession with wedding details while cheating is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: I am completely committed to Andy. I chose peonies for our wedding flowers.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Do you know how hard it is to get peonies? That's not something you do if you're having an affair with the senator.

jokes.text: are you sleeping with dwight? a little bit.
jokes.setup: Andy's direct question about the affair
jokes.punchline: Angela's hilariously minimizing response to cheating
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character work - Angela's attempt to minimize massive betrayal with casual understatement. Instant classic line.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Did you cheat on me?

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: It was just one time.

jokes.text: i mean, we were together, and then he killed sprinkles. and then we stopped, and... i don't know exactly when we started up again.
jokes.setup: Angela explaining the affair timeline
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of pet murder as relationship pause, not ending
jokes.explanation: Dark callback to Dwight killing Angela's cat - the fact that it only temporarily stopped their relationship is disturbing and funny

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: So we took a break from our relationship.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: That's when I killed Sprinkles.

jokes.text: david, here it is. my philosophy is basically this. and this is something that i live by. and i always have, and i always will. don't ever for any reason do anything to anyone for any reason ever, no matter what. no matter where, or who, or who you are with, or-- or where you are going, or-- or where you've been... ever... for any reason whatsoever.
jokes.setup: David asking Michael to explain his management philosophy
jokes.punchline: Michael's completely meaningless circular non-advice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Michael moment - sounds profound but says absolutely nothing. Perfect character work showing his ability to fill time with meaningless words.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: So Michael, what's your management philosophy?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Well, David, I think the key to being a great manager is to be a great manager. You have to lead by example, and the example you're leading by is the example of a leader. And people respect that. They see you leading, and they think, 'That guy's leading.' And that's when you know you've done your job as a leader.

jokes.text: sometimes i'll start a sentence, and i don't even know where it's going. i just hope i find it along the way. like an improv conversation. an improversation.
jokes.setup: Michael's confessional about his speaking style
jokes.punchline: His made-up word 'improversation' while describing his improvised talking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael self-awareness combined with his tendency to make up words. Meta-commentary on his own speech patterns.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm very good at improvising. I just make up words as I go along. Like 'improversation' — that's improvisation mixed with conversation.

jokes.text: so, like, missionary? i said nothing fancy.
jokes.setup: Discussion about keeping things simple (presumably about wedding)
jokes.punchline: Andy's innocent question gets sexual response from Angela
jokes.explanation: Sexual misdirection that works due to Angela's recent confession context

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: So we're keeping it simple?

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: That's what she said.

jokes.text: you know what? i'd also like to take this opportunity to thank all of you for lying to my face. and not telling me what's been going on this entire time. you are welcome.
jokes.setup: Andy sarcastically thanking office for deception
jokes.punchline: Stanley's matter-of-fact 'you are welcome' response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley response - completely unbothered by Andy's sarcasm, treating it as genuine gratitude

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Thank you all so much for the deception.

dialogue.speaker: Stanley
dialogue.line: You're welcome.

jokes.text: it's over. oh, good. she broke up with you. no. it's-- it's over between you two.
jokes.setup: Andy declaring something is over
jokes.punchline: Dwight's hopeful misunderstanding of what ended
jokes.explanation: Dwight's immediate assumption that Angela dumped Andy shows his confidence in their relationship

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: That's it. It's over.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Finally! I've been waiting for this day. So... we're together now?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: What? No! The project is over.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Oh.

jokes.text: she doesn't love you. she's marrying me. well, i don't know about that because she certainly seems to enjoy making lovemaking with me.
jokes.setup: Escalating argument between Andy and Dwight
jokes.punchline: Dwight's awkward phrasing 'making lovemaking'
jokes.explanation: Dwight's stilted sexual terminology shows his discomfort with direct language

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: You can't just waltz in here and take over my project!

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Your project? I've been nurturing this client relationship. We've been making lovemaking to this deal.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Making lovemaking? What does that even mean?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: It means we're building something intimate and beautiful, Bernard.

jokes.text: angela bernard. will never be her name. it will be her name, and you will have to call her that.
jokes.setup: Fight over Angela's future name
jokes.punchline: Andy's petty insistence on name pronunciation protocol
jokes.explanation: Andy's focus on formal address during emotional confrontation shows his privileged background

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: It's pronounced 'An-DELL', not 'An-dul'.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I've been saying it correctly for years.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: No, you haven't. And frankly, I'm tired of correcting you in front of the office.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Your pronunciation is non-standard and frankly, inaccurate.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: It's my name, Dwight. I think I know how to say it.

jokes.text: i will fight you. nope. okay, fine! good! a duel. the winner gets angela. fine! fine!
jokes.setup: Escalating masculine confrontation
jokes.punchline: Andy's leap to formal duel with Angela as prize
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation to absurdist solution - Andy's Cornell education manifesting as formal duel challenge

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: You know what, Dwight? I'm tired of your nonsense. I challenge you to a duel.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: A duel? Finally, a man of honor. Name your terms.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: The prize? Angela's hand in marriage.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Angela? You can't use Angela as a prize!

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Why not? You've basically been doing it.

jokes.text: my bare hands. that is stupid. i will use a sword, and i will cut off your bare hands. then i'll get something too.
jokes.setup: Choosing duel weapons
jokes.punchline: Dwight's logical response to Andy's disadvantageous weapon choice
jokes.explanation: Dwight's practical approach to combat combined with Andy's dawning realization of poor planning

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I choose... a hammer.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: You've made a grave mistake. I choose a gun.

jokes.text: i've had two men fight over me before. usually it's over which one gets to hold the camcorder.
jokes.setup: Angela commenting on men fighting over her
jokes.punchline: Dark implication about her past involving filming
jokes.explanation: Shocking revelation that completely changes perception of uptight Angela - brilliant dark comedy

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Well, it's not the first time two men have fought over me. The last time it happened, we were all wearing masks and there were cameras everywhere.

jokes.text: i feel the need... the need for tweed.
jokes.setup: Michael telling story about Kevin's jacket
jokes.punchline: Top Gun reference adapted for tweed jacket
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael pop culture reference with word substitution - competent but predictable

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: So Kevin shows up wearing this tweed jacket, right? And I'm like, 'Kevin, what is that?' And he goes, 'It's tweed, Michael.' So I tell him, 'You feel the need... the need for tweed!'

jokes.text: i can't believe they're gonna fight over me. i guess people have fewer choices as they get older.
jokes.setup: Angela reflecting on the duel situation
jokes.punchline: Her cold assessment that she's settling due to limited options
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest self-assessment that's both pathetic and self-aware from Angela

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: I'm settling. That's what this is. I'm settling because my options are limited and I'm not getting any younger.

jokes.text: dear dwight, by now you have received my note. how are you? i am well. you are no doubt wondering why i have left this note.
jokes.setup: Andy's formal duel challenge note
jokes.punchline: Overly polite letter format for combat challenge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy character work - his prep school manners applied to primitive challenge

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Dear Sir or Madam, I am writing to formally challenge you to a duel. I hope this letter finds you in good health and spirits. At your earliest convenience, I propose we settle our dispute through single combat. I remain, yours in courteous antagonism, Andy Bernard.

jokes.text: it's come to my attention that in any physical match with you, i would surely be bested. true. the soft underbelly of my refined upbringing is my soft underbelly.
jokes.setup: Andy admitting physical disadvantage
jokes.punchline: Redundant metaphor about his literal and figurative soft underbelly
jokes.explanation: Andy's self-aware admission of privileged weakness with awkward metaphor construction

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I know I'm not the strongest guy in the office. I mean, look at Dwight, look at me.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: You're soft. Like a soft underbelly.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: That's... that's literally what I have. A soft underbelly. So that's not really a metaphor, Dwight.

jokes.text: the prius is silent if he keeps it under five miles per hour. he deserves the win.
jokes.setup: Andy approaching in his car for surprise attack
jokes.punchline: Creed's appreciation for Andy's stealth technique
jokes.explanation: Brilliant detail about Prius stealth capability turning eco-car into weapon of surprise

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: That's how you do it. Sneak up on somebody, strike fast, get out. That's what I do.

jokes.text: protect her from what? bears, you idiot? when's the last time you saw a damn bear in scranton? last year, idiot!
jokes.setup: Andy mocking Dwight's protective abilities
jokes.punchline: Dwight's matter-of-fact confirmation of recent bear encounter
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that validates Dwight's paranoia - what seemed absurd is revealed as practical

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: False. Bears eat beets. Bears. Beets. Battlestar Galactica.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Identity theft is not a joke, Jim.

jokes.text: you're like a sasquatch. you live in the woods. sasquatches are the strongest animal on the planet! so fine, call me a sasquatch.
jokes.setup: Andy insulting Dwight's appearance and lifestyle
jokes.punchline: Dwight turning insult into compliment with questionable sasquatch facts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight response - taking insult as compliment with bizarre animal facts

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: You look like a sasquatch. Your lifestyle is basically Bigfoot—living in the woods, no hygiene, probably eating raw meat.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Actually, sasquatches are incredibly efficient hunters with excellent night vision and can run up to 40 miles per hour. So thank you for the compliment.

jokes.text: i don't get it. how could she be sleeping with you this whole time and only sleep with me twice? what? what? she's sleeping with you? i'm her fiance. she said she was only sleeping with me.
jokes.setup: Andy and Dwight comparing their relationships with Angela
jokes.punchline: Mutual realization that Angela lied to both of them
jokes.explanation: Perfect revelation moment - both men discovering they're equally deceived by Angela

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Wait, Angela told you she loved you?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Of course she did. We had something special.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Dwight, she told me the exact same thing. Last month.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: That's impossible.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I have text messages.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: She... she lied to me?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: She lied to both of us.

jokes.text: yes, hi. my last name is bernard, and i would like to cancel a wedding cake that i had ordered. b-e-r-n-a-r-d. yeah. the one shaped like a sailboat.
jokes.setup: Andy canceling wedding arrangements after discovering truth
jokes.punchline: The specific detail of sailboat-shaped cake makes the loss more poignant
jokes.explanation: Sailboat cake detail adds specific sadness - shows Andy's romantic investment and nautical interests

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I can't believe I have to cancel everything. The venue, the flowers, the band...

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: And the cake. It's a sailboat. A sailboat cake.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Who makes a sailboat cake? I made that request. That was my thing.

jokes.text: wow, what a day! ha ha! i thought i was gonna get chewed out. but hold on! here's an attaboy for you. what? roller-coaster ride. roller-coast.
jokes.setup: Michael returning from successful corporate meeting
jokes.punchline: His obliviousness to the office chaos he left behind
jokes.explanation: Michael's typical self-absorption - completely unaware of the relationship destruction he caused

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That meeting went amazing. I closed the deal, impressed corporate, and I didn't even break a sweat.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Michael, while you were gone, Jim set the copier on fire.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: What? How did that happen?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I was conducting a legitimate office safety drill.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Well, nobody told me about this. Sounds like you all handled it fine without me.

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: The sprinklers went off. Everything is soaked.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That's what sprinklers do. They're doing their job. See? The system works.

tmdb_overview: The office is on eggshells because Andy still hasn't found out about fiancee Angela's affair with Dwight. When Michael spills the beans, Dwight and Andy take matters into their own hands. Meanwhile, Michael is nervous about being called down to corporate for a meeting with Wallace.
insight: Dwight and Andy's escalating rivalry sustains 51 jokes across pure character conflict.
